repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38946

## Solution

First PR to implement a new overload to the current vim.net.request method, adding support for multiple HTTP methods

Still needs to:

- Parse response and return a new response, status fields in `vim.net.request.Response`
- Add new enum for HTTP Statuses
- Support `table` on request `body`, automatically adding the `Content-Type: application/json` Header

comment_data.body:
--- @overload ...

examples: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/eeee4bd4fc4117c994011d087b46e6e5f9a74379/runtime/lua/vim/_core/shared.lua#L1295-L1297
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -56,20 +59,61 @@ local M = {}
 --- vim.net.request('https://neovim.io/charter/', {
 ---   headers = { Authorization = 'Bearer XYZ' },
 --- })
+---
+--- -- POST request with body.
+--- vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com/api', {
+---   body = '{"key": "value"}',
+--- })
 --- ```
 ---
---- @param url string The URL for the request.
+--- @param method string The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE) or URL for GET request.
+--- @param url string The URL for the request or opts table.
 --- @param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
 --- @param on_response? fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
 --- Callback invoked on request completion. The `body` field in the response
 --- parameter contains the raw response data (text or binary).
 --- @return { close: fun() } # Object with `close()` method which cancels the request.
-function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
-  vim.validate('url', url, 'string')
-  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
-  vim.validate('on_response', on_response, 'function', true)
+function M.request(method, url, opts, on_response)

     Parameters: ~
-      • {url}          (`string`) The URL for the request.
+      • {method}       (`string`) The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH,
+                       HEAD, DELETE) or URL for GET request.

comment_data.body:
if the old signature is detected, just reassign the params, then we don't need 2 different validation blocks.

```lua
if old then
  opts = url
  url = method
  method = 'GET'
end
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -56,20 +59,61 @@ local M = {}
 --- vim.net.request('https://neovim.io/charter/', {
 ---   headers = { Authorization = 'Bearer XYZ' },
 --- })
+---
+--- -- POST request with body.
+--- vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com/api', {
+---   body = '{"key": "value"}',
+--- })
 --- ```
 ---
---- @param url string The URL for the request.
+--- @param method string The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE) or URL for GET request.
+--- @param url string The URL for the request or opts table.
 --- @param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
 --- @param on_response? fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
 --- Callback invoked on request completion. The `body` field in the response
 --- parameter contains the raw response data (text or binary).
 --- @return { close: fun() } # Object with `close()` method which cancels the request.
-function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
-  vim.validate('url', url, 'string')
-  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
-  vim.validate('on_response', on_response, 'function', true)
+function M.request(method, url, opts, on_response)
+  local http_methods = {
+    GET = true,
+    POST = true,
+    PUT = true,
+    PATCH = true,
+    HEAD = true,
+    DELETE = true,
+  }
+
+  if on_response == nil then
+    -- old behavior
+    vim.validate('url', method, 'string')
+    vim.validate('opts', url, 'table', true)
+    vim.validate('on_response', opts, 'function', true)

comment_data.body: Nit: should this be `HTTPMethod`? Not a strong opinion, just want it to be aligned with codebase and conventions
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,5 +1,8 @@
 local M = {}
 
+---@alias vim.net.request.ResponseFunc fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
+---@alias vim.net.HttpMethod string "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "PACH" | "HEAD"| "DELETE

comment_data.body:
I think this suffices and is cleaner:
```lua
  if type(url) ~= 'string' then
    on_response = opts
    opts = url
    url = method
    method = 'GET'
  end
  method = method:upper()
  opts = opts or {}


  vim.validate('method', method, function(m)
    return http_methods[m] == true, ('Invalid HTTP method: %s'):format(m)
  end)
  vim.validate('url', url, 'string')
  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
  vim.validate('opts.headers', opts.headers, 'table', true)
  vim.validate('opts.body', opts.body, 'string', true)
  vim.validate('on_response', on_response, 'function', true)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -56,20 +62,60 @@ local M = {}
 --- vim.net.request('https://neovim.io/charter/', {
 ---   headers = { Authorization = 'Bearer XYZ' },
 --- })
+---
+--- -- POST request with body.
+--- vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com/api', {
+---   body = '{"key": "value"}',
+--- })
 --- ```
 ---
+--- @param method string (default: GET) The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE).
 --- @param url string The URL for the request.
 --- @param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
---- @param on_response? fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
+--- @param on_response? vim.net.request.ResponseFunc
 --- Callback invoked on request completion. The `body` field in the response
 --- parameter contains the raw response data (text or binary).
+--- @overload fun(url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
+--- @overload fun(method: vim.net.HttpMethod, url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
 --- @return { close: fun() } # Object with `close()` method which cancels the request.
-function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
-  vim.validate('url', url, 'string')
-  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
-  vim.validate('on_response', on_response, 'function', true)
+function M.request(method, url, opts, on_response)
+  local http_methods = {
+    GET = true,
+    POST = true,
+    PUT = true,
+    PATCH = true,
+    HEAD = true,
+    DELETE = true,
+  }
+
+  if on_response == nil then
+    -- old behavior
+    local on_resp = opts
+    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: cast-local-type
+    opts = url
+    url = method
+    method = 'GET'
+    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: cast-local-type
+    on_response = on_resp
+  else
+    -- new behavior
+    vim.validate('method', method, 'string')
+    vim.validate('url', url, 'string')
+    vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
+    vim.validate('on_response', on_response, 'function', true)
+
+    opts = opts or {}
+    method = method:upper()
+
+    if not http_methods[method:upper()] then
+      error(
+        'invalid HTTP method: '
+          .. method
+          .. '. Supported methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE'
+      )
+    end
+  end

comment_data.body:
```lua
  -- curl -X HEAD does not work and advises to use --head instead
  vim.list_extend(args, method == 'HEAD' and { '--head' } or { '-X', method })
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -85,6 +131,15 @@ function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
     vim.list_extend(args, { '--output', opts.outpath })
   end
 
+  if method == 'HEAD' then
+    table.insert(args, '-I')
+  elseif vim.tbl_contains({ 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH' }, method) then

comment_data.body:
Might want to do some validation here. Probably also more self-documenting to use the longer form flags:

```lua
  if opts.body then
    if key:match('^@') then
      error('request body is not allowed to start with "@"')
    end
    vim.list_extend(args, { '--data-binary', opts.body })
  end
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -85,6 +131,15 @@ function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
     vim.list_extend(args, { '--output', opts.outpath })
   end
 
+  if method == 'HEAD' then
+    table.insert(args, '-I')
+  elseif vim.tbl_contains({ 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH' }, method) then
+    if opts.body ~= nil then
+      vim.validate('opts.body', opts.body, 'string')
+      vim.list_extend(args, { '-d', opts.body })
+    end

-                       response data (text or binary).

+                         response data (text or binary).

comment_data.body: not in this PR, but in a followup we should consider sending body as stdin (`--data-binary @-`) since it can exceed the max arg size?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -85,8 +125,16 @@ function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
     vim.list_extend(args, { '--output', opts.outpath })
   end
 
+  -- curl -X HEAD does not work and advises to use --head instead
+  vim.list_extend(args, method == 'HEAD' and { '--head' } or { '--request', method })
+
+  if vim.tbl_contains({ 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH' }, method) then
+    if opts.body then
+      vim.list_extend(args, { '--data-binary', opts.body })

comment_data.body: it looks like https://httpbingo.org/ supports post requests. can we have an integ test that exercises that? i don't think we need to test every method, but at least exercise POST with some body data.

That's a good point and a 2-line change:

```suggestion
      vim.list_extend(args, { '--data-binary', '@-' })
```
and
```diff
-  local job = vim.system(args, {}, function(res)
+  local job = vim.system(args, { stdin = opts.body }, function(res)
```

(Okay plus test updates)

comment_data.body: I think it would be nice to include `headers = { ['Content-Type'] = 'application/json' }` here to hint people to set the right content type (especially with json data).
Also why have `vim.net.HttpMethod` defined on line 4 when you're not using it here? Either remove the alias or use it here

 --- ```
 ---
+--- @param method string (default: GET) The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE).
 --- @param url string The URL for the request.
 --- @param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
---- @param on_response? fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
---- Callback invoked on request completion. The `body` field in the response
---- parameter contains the raw response data (text or binary).
+--- @param on_response? vim.net.request.ResponseFunc
+--- Callback invoked on request completion. Contains two parameters:
+--- - The response callback `error` string, if any.
+--- - The `body` field in the response with the raw response data (text or binary).
+--- @overload fun(url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
+--- @overload fun(method: vim.net.HttpMethod, url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
 --- @return { close: fun() } # Object with `close()` method which cancels the request.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -56,20 +71,43 @@ local M = {}
 --- vim.net.request('https://neovim.io/charter/', {
 ---   headers = { Authorization = 'Bearer XYZ' },
 --- })
+---
+--- -- POST request with body.
+--- vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com/api', {
+---   body = '{"key": "value"}',
+---   headers = {['Content-Type'] = 'application/json' }
+--- })
 --- ```
 ---
+--- @param method vim.net.HttpMethod (default: GET) The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE).
 --- @param url string The URL for the request.
 --- @param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
---- @param on_response? fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
---- Callback invoked on request completion. The `body` field in the response
---- parameter contains the raw response data (text or binary).
+--- @param on_response? vim.net.request.ResponseFunc Callback invoked on request completion.
+--- @overload fun(url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
+--- @overload fun(method: vim.net.HttpMethod, url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
 --- @return { close: fun() } # Object with `close()` method which cancels the request.
-function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
+function M.request(method, url, opts, on_response)
+  if type(url) ~= 'string' then
+    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: cast-local-type
+    on_response = opts
+    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: no-unknown
+    opts = url

@@ -56,20 +71,43 @@ local M = {}
 --- vim.net.request('https://neovim.io/charter/', {
 ---   headers = { Authorization = 'Bearer XYZ' },
 --- })
+---
+--- -- POST request with body.
+--- vim.net.request('POST', 'https://example.com/api', {
+---   body = '{"key": "value"}',
+---   headers = {['Content-Type'] = 'application/json' }
+--- })
 --- ```
 ---
+--- @param method vim.net.HttpMethod (default: GET) The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, DELETE).
 --- @param url string The URL for the request.
 --- @param opts? vim.net.request.Opts
---- @param on_response? fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
---- Callback invoked on request completion. The `body` field in the response
---- parameter contains the raw response data (text or binary).
+--- @param on_response? vim.net.request.ResponseFunc Callback invoked on request completion.
+--- @overload fun(url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
+--- @overload fun(method: vim.net.HttpMethod, url: string, opts: vim.net.request.Opts, response: vim.net.request.ResponseFunc)
 --- @return { close: fun() } # Object with `close()` method which cancels the request.
-function M.request(url, opts, on_response)
+function M.request(method, url, opts, on_response)
+  if type(url) ~= 'string' then
+    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: cast-local-type
+    on_response = opts
+    ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: no-unknown
+    opts = url
+    url = method
+    method = 'GET'
+  end
+  opts = opts or {}
+
+  vim.validate('method', method, function(m)
+    return http_methods[m] == true, ('invalid HTTP method: %s'):format(m)
+  end)
   vim.validate('url', url, 'string')
   vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
+  vim.validate('opts.headers', opts.headers, 'table', true)
+  vim.validate('opts.body', opts.body, function(b)
+    return (b == nil and true) or (type(b) == 'string' and not b:match('^@'))
+  end, true, 'body should be string and not start with @')

comment_data.body:
There are a couple typos:
- should be "PATCH", not "PACH"
- missing closing quotation mark for DELETE
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,5 +1,17 @@
 local M = {}
 
+local http_methods = {
+  GET = true,
+  POST = true,
+  PUT = true,
+  PATCH = true,
+  HEAD = true,
+  DELETE = true,
+}
+
+---@alias vim.net.request.ResponseFunc fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
+---@alias vim.net.HttpMethod string "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "PACH" | "HEAD"| "DELETE

comment_data.body:
This makes the first argument optional through overloading, which is a bit outside the existing pattern since optional arguments are usually at the end.

The documentation here gives me the impression that even if I want to keep `method` as the default value, I still need to pass `nil` as a placeholder, i.e., `vim.net.request(nil, url, opts)`. Though the examples above are useful, I think it would be better to provide more explanation in the documentation.
There's a good chance the method argument will be required at some point, or convenience functions like `vim.net.get` etc will be introduced. Butv your point is still valid; docs could be improved
Nice catch! Will fix it
comment_data.diff_hunk:

+local http_methods = {

+  DELETE = true,
+}
+
+---@alias vim.net.request.ResponseFunc fun(err: string?, response: vim.net.request.Response?)
+---@alias vim.net.HttpMethod string "GET" | "POST" | "PUT" | "PACH" | "HEAD"| "DELETE

comment_data.body: @ofseed @yochem does adding a `method?` helps on showing this field is optional?
probably not. if this becomes complicated , let's not spend time on it. the overload mostly for back-compat, though it is a nice ergonomic for the GET case.

comment_regular_data.body: Than you so much for the awesome review and feedback as always @justinmk and @yochem
